mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) reflect sustained consumer expenditure that drives corporate revenue growth and earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns do not occur in more than 55 percent of months with rising RSAFS during any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above 5 percent increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and raises debt service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 does not produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of quarters when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5 percent over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in industrial production and increase in unemployment captures a contractionary aggregate demand shock that compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint deterioration in INDPRO and UNRATE fails to coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of observed months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a housing correction reduce residential construction activity and household wealth accumulation, lowering aggregate demand and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day S&P 500 returns do not follow three consecutive monthly declines in HOUST in more than 50 percent of episodes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month span signals weakening labor income and softening aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases of more than 0.3 percentage points do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High federal funds rates combined with decelerating retail sales indicate restrictive monetary policy constraining the consumer sector, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high FEDFUNDS and decelerating RSAFS does not coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of weeks over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals weakening goods-sector output and inventory accumulation pressures, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation during an elevated inflation regime causes the market to discount tighter future monetary policy and erodes real earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Accelerating CPIAUCSL above 3 percent annualized does not produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of quarters over a 3-year rolling window.
